title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: 03-31-2026
content:
Northern Valley remains compliant in most areas with board-approved policy limits, supported by strong asset quality and capital metrics.

Asset Quality:
Classified assets/net worth of 0.02% remain below the limit of <40%, while delinquent loans/total loans of 0.00% remain within the threshold of <1.5%. Net charge-offs / average loans of 0.00% also remain within the best practice range of <0.75%.

Capital & Solvency:
Net worth ratio remains a key strength, with net worth/total assets at 0.14 (or 14%) and solvency evaluation at 1.17 both remaining above their respective policy benchmarks of >=10% and >=7%. Net worth growth of 0.07 (or 7%) also remains within the target range of >=5% YoY.

Profitability & Growth:
Profitability metrics remain generally favorable, with net margin/average assets at 0.05 (or 5%), return on average assets at 0.01 (or 1%), and operating expenses / average assets at 0.04 (or 4%). However, loan growth of -0.38% falls below the target range of 5%-10%, indicating an area to monitor.

Interest Rate & Funding:
Liquidity and funding metrics remain within preferred ranges for most categories: cost of funds / average assets at 0.01 (or 1%), net interest margin/average assets at 0.04 (or 4%), yield on average loans at 0.06 (or 6%), cash and short-term investments / total assets at 0.13 (or 13%), total loans / total assets at 0.66 (or 66%), and total loans / total shares at 0.77 (or 77%). However, regular shares/total shares & borrowings of 0.52 falls below the minimum target of >=70%, while membership growth of 0.06 remains within the range of 3%-7%.

Northern Valley demonstrates strong asset quality and capital adequacy but faces challenges in loan growth and funding mix, requiring strategic adjustments to align with policy targets.
